# Grow a Garden 2 Guides & Tools

## Overview
A Next.js (App Router, static export) fan site for the Roblox game Grow a Garden 2 (GAG2), developed by Strawberreh Squad / Splitting Point Studios. It provides crop values, mutation multipliers, active codes, seed prices, pets, gear, and night-stealing guides — all community-data-driven and updated daily. This is an unofficial fan site, not affiliated with Roblox Corporation or the developers.

## Tech Stack
- Next.js (App Router, `output: export` static export)
- React + TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS
- Framer Motion (animations)
- react-icons (Feather icon set)

## Project Structure
- `/src/app` — pages and routes (App Router)
- `/src/components` — reusable UI components
- `/src/lib/games.config.ts` — single config file driving the whole site (game name, SEO, pages, features, FAQs, calculator)
- `/src/data` — JSON data files (`items.json` = 36 crops, `codes.json` = active codes, `game.config.json` = config mirror)
- `/public` — static assets (favicons, crop images, manifest, robots)

## Key Pages
- `/` — Home (Hero, Features, HowTo, Why, FAQ, Share)
- `/calculator` — Profit calculator (Sell = BaseValue × weight² × Mutation × FriendBoost × Quantity)
- `/codes` — Active codes (TEAMGREENBEAN)
- `/tier-list` — 36 crops ranked by GAG2 rarity (Super → Common), each with a detail page
- `/beginner-guide` — Quick start walkthrough
- `/mutations` — 7 confirmed mutation multipliers + weather triggers
- `/pets` — Pet catalog and spawn mechanics
- `/gear` — Sprinklers, defense, raid tools
- `/seeds` — Seed shop prices and restock odds
- `/weather` — 10 weather events and their mutation triggers
- `/night-stealing` — Day/night cycle and defense strategies
- `/updates`, `/about`, `/privacy`, `/disclaimer`

## Configuration
Everything is config-driven from `src/lib/games.config.ts`. To rebrand for another game, replace the `defaultConfig` fields and the data JSON files — no component changes required.

## Build & Deploy
Static export (`next.config.mjs` → `output: "export"`), deployable to Cloudflare Pages or any static host. Build with `npm run build`; output lands in `out/`.
